_meta.description: UNFPA OTG Field App Drug Formulary — comprehensive reproductive health essential medicines
_meta.note: Expanded formulary includes WHO EML essential medicines and UNFPA reproductive health protocols. All entries must be verified by a qualified clinician and approved by the Clinical Advisory Panel before clinical deployment. Source citations reference exact page numbers.

categories.supportive: Antibiotics, pain relief, and supportive care

sources: WHO Model List of Essential Medicines 23rd edition (2023)
WHO PCPNC — Pregnancy, Childbirth, Postpartum and Newborn Care: A guide for essential practice (3rd ed, 2023)
WHO IMPAC/MCPC — Managing Complications in Pregnancy and Childbirth (2nd ed, 2017)
WHO Medical Eligibility Criteria for Contraceptive Use (MEC) 5th edition (2015)
WHO Pocket Handbook for Maternal Mortality Review (2011)
UNFPA Technical Guidance on Contraceptive Methods (2023)

localNames.my: အောက်စီတိုစင်
localNames.km: អុកស៊ីតូស៊ីន

drugs.indication: Active management of third stage of labour (AMTSL); prevention and treatment of postpartum haemorrhage (PPH)
drugs.dose: 10 IU
drugs.timing: Within 1 minute of delivery of the baby, before delivery of the placenta
drugs.alternativeDose: For PPH treatment: 20–40 IU in 1 litre IV fluids at 40 drops/min; or 10 IU IM if IV access not available
contraindications: Situations where vaginal delivery is inadvisable
warnings: Do not give as rapid IV bolus — may cause hypotension and cardiac arrhythmia
Has antidiuretic effect — risk of water intoxication with prolonged use in large volumes
Store at 2–8°C; loses potency above 25°C — in hot climates, prefer misoprostol if cold chain unreliable
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023, Section C3 (Preventing postpartum haemorrhage), Page 89

localNames.km: មីស្សូប្រូស្តូល

drugs.indication: Prevention of PPH where oxytocin unavailable or cold chain unreliable; cervical ripening; early pregnancy failure; incomplete abortion
drugs.dose: 600 mcg
drugs.timing: Immediately after delivery of the baby
drugs.alternativeDose: PPH treatment (when oxytocin unavailable): 800 mcg sublingually. Cervical priming: 25 mcg vaginally q6h (hospital setting only).
contraindications: Previous uterine scar with >1 prior caesarean (for cervical ripening doses)
warnings: Side effects: fever, shivering, nausea, diarrhoea — inform patient before use
Higher doses (800 mcg SL) for PPH treatment have more side effects — clinical supervision required
Do not use sublingual doses for PPH prevention in facility settings when oxytocin is available
Legal context varies by country — verify local regulations before use for abortion indications
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023, Section C3; WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed. 2017

localNames.my: မဂ္ဂနီဆီယမ် ဆာလဖိတ်
localNames.km: ម៉ាញ៉េស្យូម ស៊ុលហ្វាត

drugs.indication: Prevention and treatment of eclamptic seizures; severe pre-eclampsia
drugs.dose: Loading: 4 g IV over 5–10 minutes, then 1 g/hour IV maintenance
drugs.route: IV (or IM if IV access unavailable — see alternative)
drugs.timing: Start immediately on diagnosis of severe pre-eclampsia or eclampsia
drugs.alternativeDose: IM loading (if no IV): 5 g IM each buttock (= 10 g total) + 2.5 g IM q4h. Continue for 24 hours after last seizure or delivery.
contraindications: Urine output < 25 mL/hour in preceding 4 hours
warnings: Have calcium gluconate 1 g IV ready as antidote for magnesium toxicity
Monitor: respiratory rate, patellar reflexes, and urine output every 30 minutes
Signs of toxicity: absent patellar reflex, respiratory rate < 12/min — STOP infusion immediately
Antidote: calcium gluconate 1 g IV (10 mL of 10% solution) over 10 minutes
drugs.source: WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed. 2017, Section S-55 (Eclampsia)

localNames.my: အာမောဇဆလင်
localNames.km: អាម៉ុកស៊ីស៊ីលីន

drugs.indication: Group B streptococcal prophylaxis in labour; treatment of urinary tract infection in pregnancy; postpartum infection (in combination)
drugs.dose: 500 mg
drugs.timing: Every 8 hours; duration depends on indication
drugs.alternativeDose: IV: 500 mg–1 g q6–8h for severe infection. For GBS prophylaxis in labour: 2 g IV initial dose, then 1 g IV q4h until delivery.
contraindications: Known penicillin allergy (anaphylaxis) — use erythromycin or clindamycin
warnings: Check for penicillin allergy history before prescribing
For women with penicillin allergy: use local antibiogram-guided alternative
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023; WHO EML 23rd edition (2023)

localNames.my: သံဓာတ်တံဆိပ်
localNames.km: ជាតិដែក និងអាស៊ីតហ្វូលិក

drugs.indication: Prevention and treatment of iron-deficiency anaemia in pregnancy and postpartum
drugs.dose: 60 mg elemental iron + 400 mcg folic acid
drugs.timing: Once daily throughout pregnancy and 3 months postpartum (prevention). Twice daily for treatment of anaemia.
contraindications: Anaemia not due to iron deficiency (investigate cause)
warnings: Take on empty stomach for best absorption; may cause nausea — take with food if not tolerated
Stools will turn dark — inform the patient this is expected and harmless
For severe anaemia (Hb < 7 g/dL in pregnancy): refer for further assessment
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016, Recommendation A2 (Iron and folic acid supplementation)

localNames.my: လီভိုနော်ဂျက်စတရယ်
localNames.km: លីវូណូហ្គេស្ត្រល

drugs.indication: Emergency contraception (Plan B); regular oral contraceptive (mini-pill)
drugs.dose: 1.5 mg (emergency, single dose); 30 mcg daily (regular pill)
drugs.timing: Within 72 hours of unprotected intercourse (emergency); daily at same time (regular)
drugs.alternativeDose: Can be taken as 2 doses of 0.75 mg within 12 hours, though single dose preferred
contraindications: Current or history of breast cancer (relative — risk-benefit assessment needed)
warnings: Emergency use: NOT a regular contraceptive — counsel on ongoing family planning
Regular use: no protection against STIs — counsel on dual method use
Ectopic pregnancy risk is similar to baseline; high suspicion warranted if pregnant
drugs.source: UNFPA Technical Guidance on Contraceptive Methods 2023; WHO MEC 5th ed. 2015

localNames.my: မိုင်ဖပ်ရီစ်တုံး
localNames.km: មីសេផ្រីស្តូន

drugs.indication: Medical abortion (in pregnancy <13 weeks); preparation for surgical abortion; molar pregnancy; ectopic pregnancy monitoring
drugs.dose: 200–600 mg (varies by protocol and gestation)
drugs.timing: Gestation-dependent; requires strict clinical supervision
drugs.alternativeDose: Low-dose: 200 mg oral, then misoprostol 400 mcg PV/SL at 24–48 hours (WHO recommended)
contraindications: Known or suspected ectopic pregnancy (until ruled out)
Uncontrolled asthma; bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use
warnings: Requires informed consent and counselling on medical abortion process
Heavy bleeding and passage of products — counsel on when to seek help
Follow-up ultrasound at 3–5 days to confirm complete abortion
Legal status varies widely by country — verify local regulations and ethical oversight before use
RhD status must be assessed; RhD-negative women need anti-D immunoglobulin
drugs.source: WHO Abortion Care Guideline (2022); UNFPA guidance on reproductive rights

localNames.my: စက်ထရီယက်စုံ
localNames.km: សេហ្វត្រីយាក់សូន

drugs.indication: Gonorrhoea treatment (first-line); pelvic inflammatory disease; postpartum infection; neonatal ophthalmia prophylaxis
drugs.dose: 250 mg IM (single dose for gonorrhoea); 1–2 g IV/IM q12h for systemic infection
drugs.timing: Immediate (gonorrhoea treatment)
drugs.alternativeDose: For PID: 1 g IV/IM q12–24h + doxycycline. Neonatal eye prophylaxis: 50 mg/mL solution, 1 drop in each eye once.
contraindications: History of severe penicillin allergy (assess cross-reactivity)
warnings: Combination therapy for PID (add doxycycline or azithromycin)
For neonates: preferred agent for ophthalmia prophylaxis and treatment of gonococcal infection
Resistance emerging in some regions — monitor local antibiograms
drugs.source: WHO STI Treatment Guidelines 2022; WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: အဇီသြိုမိုင်စင်
localNames.km: អាស៊ីស្ទ្រូមីស៊ីន

drugs.indication: Chlamydial infection; part of PID regimen; azithromycin-resistant gonorrhoea; macrolide alternative for penicillin allergy
drugs.dose: 1 g oral (single dose for chlamydia); 500 mg daily for 3 days (alternative)
drugs.timing: Single 1 g dose preferred for chlamydia in pregnancy and postpartum
contraindications: Concurrent use with certain cardiac drugs (QT prolongation risk)
warnings: Safe in pregnancy and breastfeeding
Gastrointestinal side effects common — counsel patient
For gonorrhoea: use in combination with ceftriaxone, not monotherapy
drugs.source: WHO STI Treatment Guidelines 2022; WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023

localNames.my: ဒောက်ဆီကလိုင်း
localNames.km: ដុកស៊ីស៊ីលីន

drugs.indication: Chlamydial infection (non-pregnant women); part of PID regimen; non-gonococcal urethritis; syphilis alternative
drugs.dose: 100 mg twice daily for 7 days (chlamydia); higher doses for PID
drugs.timing: Twice daily with food
contraindications: Pregnancy and breastfeeding (risk of permanent tooth staining in fetus/infant)
warnings: CONTRAINDICATED IN PREGNANCY — ensure negative pregnancy test or use reliable contraception
Photosensitivity — counsel on sun protection
GI upset — take with food, but not with milk/antacids (reduced absorption)
drugs.source: WHO STI Treatment Guidelines 2022

localNames.my: ဘင်ဇာတင်းပီနီစီလင်း
localNames.km: ប៉េនីស៊ីលីនជីបេនហាទីន

drugs.indication: Early syphilis (primary, secondary, early latent); congenital syphilis prevention (maternal treatment); yaws
drugs.dose: 2.4 million units IM (single dose for early syphilis); adjusted for weight in neonates
drugs.timing: Single dose; can be repeated weekly × 3 for late latent syphilis
drugs.alternativeDose: Late latent syphilis: 2.4 MU weekly × 3 weeks. Congenital syphilis (neonatal): 50,000 units/kg IV q12h × 10 days
contraindications: Anaphylaxis to penicillins (assess with caution in pregnancy due to serious complications of untreated syphilis)
warnings: Jarisch-Herxheimer reaction common in early syphilis — counsel patient on expected fever, rash, arthralgias lasting 24 hours
Safe in pregnancy for maternal and congenital syphilis
Partner notification and treatment essential
Monitor for penicillin allergy — desensitization may be needed in pregnant women
drugs.source: WHO STI Treatment Guidelines 2022; WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: ဗီတာမင်-K
localNames.km: វីតាមីនខ

drugs.indication: Prevention of vitamin K deficiency bleeding (VKDB) in neonates
drugs.dose: 1 mg IM (newborns); 2 mg IM (infants >8 weeks with breastfeeding risk factor)
drugs.route: IM (preferred); oral if IM refused (requires follow-up doses at 2–4 weeks and 6–8 weeks)
drugs.timing: Within first hour of life
drugs.alternativeDose: Oral: 2 mg at birth, 2–4 mg at 3–5 days, 2 mg at 4 weeks (for breastfed infants)
warnings: IM route preferred (single dose, high compliance)
Oral route requires 3 doses — counsel caregivers on importance of follow-up
No contraindication in infants with isoimmune haemolysis
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023, Section F (Newborn and Infant Health); AAP Recommendations

localNames.my: ငွေ-နိုင်ထရိတ်
localNames.km: ទឹក perok nitrat

drugs.indication: Neonatal ophthalmia prophylaxis (gonococcal and chlamydial prevention); conjunctivitis treatment in resource-limited settings
drugs.dose: 1 drop of 1% solution in each eye
drugs.timing: Within 1 hour of birth
drugs.alternativeDose: Ceftriaxone 50 mg/mL ophthalmic drops preferred in well-equipped facilities. Tetracycline 1% or erythromycin 0.5% eye ointment acceptable alternatives.
contraindications: None — universally indicated for all neonates
warnings: Causes mild chemical conjunctivitis lasting 24–48 hours — counsel parents
Effectiveness depends on proper instillation technique
Not a substitute for maternal antenatal screening and treatment
Store away from light; solution should be clear (discard if discolored)
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023, Section F; WHO Guidelines on Neonatal Infection

localNames.my: အર်ဂော့တ်-ပစ္စည်းများ
localNames.km: អាលកាឡូអ័រ ergot

drugs.indication: Management of third stage of labour (uterine contraction); prevention and treatment of postpartum haemorrhage (adjunct to oxytocin)
drugs.dose: 0.2 mg IM (methylergonovine); dose-dependent on route
drugs.route: IM, IV, or oral (avoid IV rapid bolus due to severe hypertension risk)
drugs.timing: After delivery of baby and before or after delivery of placenta (depends on protocol)
drugs.alternativeDose: Ergotamine tartrate 0.5 mg sublingual; methylergonovine 0.2 mg IM
contraindications: Coronary artery disease; severe peripheral vascular disease
warnings: NEVER give IV as bolus — risk of severe hypertension and cerebral haemorrhage
Contraindicated in twins until second baby is delivered
Third stage management: oxytocin is preferred first-line agent
Ergot alkaloids reserved for when oxytocin unavailable or ineffective
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023, Section C3; WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed. 2017

localNames.my: ဒိုင်ကလိုဖီနက်
localNames.km: ឌីក្លូហ្វេណាក់

drugs.indication: Pain relief (moderate); postpartum pain and cramping; dysmenorrhoea
drugs.dose: 50 mg IM/IV (acute pain); 50 mg orally q6–8h (up to 150 mg/day)
drugs.timing: Every 6–8 hours as needed
contraindications: Active peptic ulcer disease or gastrointestinal bleeding
warnings: First and second trimester use generally safe; AVOID third trimester
NSAID use in breastfeeding: minimal excretion in milk; generally considered safe
Risk of renal impairment in severely dehydrated women or those with underlying renal disease
Gastrointestinal upset — take with food if oral route used
drugs.source: WHO EML 23rd edition (2023); WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: ပါရာစီတမောလ်
localNames.km: ប៉ារ៉ាសេតាម៉ូល

drugs.indication: Pain relief (mild to moderate); fever reduction in pregnancy and postpartum
drugs.dose: 500–1000 mg orally q4–6h (max 4 g/day); 15–20 mg/kg per dose in children
drugs.route: Oral or rectal (rarely IV in hospital settings)
drugs.timing: Every 4–6 hours as needed
warnings: Safe in pregnancy and breastfeeding across all trimesters
First-line pain relief and antipyretic in pregnancy
Do not exceed 4 g/day due to hepatotoxicity risk
Combination products may contain multiple sources of paracetamol — counsel on reading labels
drugs.source: WHO EML 23rd edition (2023); WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: နိုင်ဖယ်ဒီပင်း
localNames.km: នីហ្វេឌីភីន

drugs.indication: Hypertensive emergency in pregnancy (severe pre-eclampsia); acute hypertensive crisis; coronary vasospasm
drugs.dose: 10 mg orally or sublingually; repeat q20 min if needed (max 40 mg); or 20 mg ER daily
drugs.route: Oral, sublingual (immediate release for acute), or IV (in hospital)
drugs.timing: Immediate for hypertensive emergency; daily for maintenance
drugs.alternativeDose: Extended release: 20–60 mg daily. IV: 0.25 mcg/kg/min, titrate up to 1.25 mcg/kg/min.
warnings: Sublingual immediate-release: less predictable than oral; preferred for acute emergency in resource-limited settings
Flushing, headache, reflex tachycardia common
Safe in pregnancy; widely used for hypertensive urgency/emergency
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016; WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed.

localNames.my: မက်သီးလ်ဒိုပါ
localNames.km: មេធីលដូផា

drugs.indication: Chronic hypertension in pregnancy; maintenance therapy for pre-eclampsia after acute phase
drugs.dose: 250 mg q8h orally; max 3 g/day; or 500 mg–1 g IV/IM q4–6h for acute control
drugs.timing: Every 8 hours; can take 3–7 days for full effect
warnings: Safest antihypertensive in pregnancy (longest safety record)
Drowsiness, fatigue common — counsel on activity safety
Positive Coombs' test (rarely causes hemolytic anemia)
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: လေဘေတောလ်
localNames.km: លាបេតាលូល

drugs.indication: Severe hypertension in pregnancy (IV for emergencies); maintenance of chronic hypertension
drugs.dose: 20 mg IV initially, then 40–80 mg q10 min (max 220 mg); or 200 mg q12h orally
drugs.timing: Every 10 minutes IV; every 12 hours oral
warnings: Combines alpha and beta blockade — effective for hypertensive emergencies
IV infusion preferred for controlled reduction (1 mg/min)
Safe in pregnancy and breastfeeding
drugs.source: WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed. 2017; WHO ANC Recommendations

localNames.my: ဟိုင်ဒရာဇင်း
localNames.km: ហ៊ីដ្រាលាស៊ីន

drugs.indication: Hypertensive emergency in pregnancy (IV); postpartum hypertensive crisis
drugs.dose: 5–10 mg IV/IM q20 min until control (max 20 mg/dose); or 10–50 mg q4–6h orally
drugs.timing: Every 20 minutes IV; every 4–6 hours oral
warnings: Reflex tachycardia and increased cardiac output — often combined with beta-blocker
Safe in pregnancy but less predictable than nifedipine or labetalol
Lupus-like syndrome with prolonged use (rare)
drugs.source: WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed. 2017

localNames.my: အမ်လိုဒီပင်း
localNames.km: អាម់លូឌីផីន

drugs.indication: Chronic hypertension in pregnancy; maintenance therapy after acute hypertensive episodes
drugs.dose: 5–10 mg once daily orally
drugs.timing: Once daily
warnings: Longer-acting than nifedipine immediate-release (12–24 hour duration)
Generally well-tolerated; less reflex tachycardia than short-acting dihydropyridines
Safe in pregnancy for chronic management
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: ဒက်ဆာမက်သေဆုံး
localNames.km: ដេក្សាមេតាសូន

drugs.indication: Fetal lung maturity acceleration (preterm labour 24–34 weeks); cerebral edema; severe asthma/allergic reactions
drugs.dose: 6 mg IM q24h × 4 doses (or 2 doses); or 0.5–1 mg IV for cerebral edema
drugs.timing: Every 24 hours for 4 doses (preterm labour); stat for emergencies
drugs.alternativeDose: Alternative to betamethasone for fetal lung maturation if unavailable
warnings: Standard dose for preterm labour: 6 mg IM Q24H × 4 doses (preferred over single course of betamethasone)
Maternal hyperglycemia common — monitor glucose especially in diabetics
Improves neonatal outcomes: reduces RDS, IVH, NEC by ~30%
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023; Cochrane review on antenatal corticosteroids

localNames.my: ဘီတာမေသေဆုံး
localNames.km: បេតាមេតាសូន

drugs.indication: Fetal lung maturity acceleration (preterm labour 24–34 weeks); reduces neonatal respiratory distress syndrome
drugs.dose: 12 mg IM q24h × 2 doses
drugs.timing: At 12 and 24 hours (standard: 2-dose course)
drugs.alternativeDose: Can use dexamethasone 6 mg IM Q24H × 4 doses as alternative
warnings: Single course most effective; repeated courses not recommended due to long-term neurodevelopmental concerns
Maternal hyperglycemia — monitor glucose in diabetics
Administer as soon as possible after preterm labour diagnosis for maximum fetal benefit
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023; Cochrane systematic review

localNames.km: នាលក់សូន

drugs.indication: Reversal of opioid-induced respiratory depression in newborns; maternal opioid overdose
drugs.dose: 0.1 mg/kg IV/IO/IM (neonatal); 0.4–2 mg IV for maternal overdose
drugs.timing: Stat for respiratory depression
drugs.alternativeDose: Neonatal: may repeat q2–3 min if no response (max 5 doses)
warnings: Does NOT reverse bradycardia — continue PPV if needed
Short half-life (30–90 min) — may need repeat dosing or infusion
Only indicated if maternal opioid use documented or suspected
drugs.source: NRP 8th edition; WHO Neonatal Guidelines

localNames.my: ဂျင်တမိုင်စင်
localNames.km: ចិនតាមីស៊ីន

drugs.indication: Neonatal sepsis (gram-negative coverage); conjunctivitis; postpartum infection prophylaxis
drugs.dose: 7.5 mg/kg IV/IM q24h (newborn); 80 mg IM q8h (maternal postpartum)
drugs.timing: Every 24 hours for neonates; every 8 hours for maternal treatment
drugs.alternativeDose: Can adjust by renal function; use therapeutic drug monitoring if available
warnings: Monitor renal function (U&Es, creatinine)
Ototoxicity risk with prolonged use — monitor hearing
Safe in pregnancy for maternal infection; readily crosses placenta but minimal fetal toxicity
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023; WHO EML 23rd edition

localNames.my: အမ်ပီစီလင်း
localNames.km: អាំពីស៊ីលីន

drugs.indication: Neonatal sepsis (gram-positive coverage); group B streptococcal meningitis; maternal GBS prophylaxis in labour
drugs.dose: 50 mg/kg IV/IM q12h (newborn); 2 g IV q4h maternal intrapartum GBS prophylaxis
drugs.timing: Every 12 hours neonatal; every 4 hours maternal intrapartum
drugs.alternativeDose: Neonatal: higher doses (100 mg/kg) for meningitis
warnings: Check penicillin allergy history — anaphylaxis is absolute contraindication
Diarrhea common — reassure caregivers
Safe in pregnancy and breastfeeding
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023; WHO EML 23rd edition

localNames.my: မက်ထရိုနီဒေဆုံး
localNames.km: មេត្រូនីដាហ្សូល

drugs.indication: Bacterial vaginosis; trichomoniasis; anaerobic infection prophylaxis; C. difficile colitis
drugs.dose: 500 mg orally q8h × 7 days (BV); 2 g single dose (trichomoniasis)
drugs.timing: Every 8 hours for 7 days; or single 2 g dose
drugs.alternativeDose: IV: 500 mg q6–8h for systemic infection; vaginal: 500 mg tablets × 7 days
warnings: Metallic taste — counsel patient
Disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol — advise abstinence
Second/third trimester safe for trichomoniasis and BV; first trimester use acceptable if benefits outweigh risks
drugs.source: WHO STI Guidelines 2022; WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: ကွန်ဆီပ်ရိုဖလောက်စီန်း
localNames.km: ស៊ីប្រូហ្វ្លក់សាស៊ីន

drugs.indication: Urinary tract infection; chancroid (genital ulcer disease); cystitis; alternative for gonococcal resistance
drugs.dose: 500 mg orally q12h × 3 days (cystitis); 500 mg single dose or q12h × 3 days (chancroid)
drugs.timing: Every 12 hours for 3 days
warnings: AVOID in pregnancy — use for cystitis only if other options unavailable
Photosensitivity — advise sun protection
Tendon rupture risk — counsel on warning signs
drugs.source: WHO STI Guidelines 2022; WHO EML 23rd edition

localNames.my: စက်ဖစ်ဆင်း
localNames.km: សេហ្វីកស៊ីម

drugs.indication: Gonorrhoea treatment (oral alternative to ceftriaxone IM); UTI; respiratory tract infection
drugs.dose: 400 mg orally single dose (gonorrhoea) or q12h × 7 days (other infections)
drugs.timing: Single dose or every 12 hours
warnings: Single 400 mg dose for uncomplicated gonorrhoea (CDC/WHO)
Combination therapy recommended (add azithromycin 1 g)
Resistance emerging in some regions — monitor local antibiograms
drugs.source: WHO STI Guidelines 2022

localNames.my: ဖလူကိုណာဆုံး
localNames.km: ហ្វ្លូកូណាហ្សូល

drugs.indication: Vaginal candidiasis (yeast infection); oral candidiasis (thrush); systemic fungal infection
drugs.dose: 150 mg orally single dose (vaginal candidiasis); 200 mg daily × 10–14 days (systemic)
drugs.timing: Single dose or daily
contraindications: Severe renal impairment without dosing adjustment
warnings: Single 150 mg dose effective for uncomplicated vaginal candidiasis
Generally safe in pregnancy; discuss risk-benefit
Drug interactions with certain contraceptives — counsel on backup method
drugs.source: WHO EML 23rd edition 2023; Cochrane review on candidiasis

localNames.km: គ្លូត្រីមាហ្សូល

drugs.indication: Vaginal candidiasis (topical); oral thrush; tinea infection; adjunct treatment of candidiasis
drugs.dose: 100 mg vaginal tablet q12h × 3 days; or 500 mg single vaginal tablet
drugs.timing: Every 12 hours × 3 days or single dose
warnings: Topical formulation — minimal systemic absorption
Safe in pregnancy (all trimesters) — preferred for vaginal candidiasis in pregnancy
May weaken latex condoms — counsel on alternative contraception
drugs.source: WHO EML 23rd edition 2023

localNames.my: တီနီဒေဆုံး
localNames.km: ទីនីដាហ្សូល

drugs.indication: Trichomoniasis; amebic dysentery; giardiasis (alternative to metronidazole)
drugs.dose: 2 g orally single dose (trichomoniasis); 2 g daily × 3 days (amebic dysentery)
drugs.timing: Single dose or daily × 3 days
warnings: Longer half-life than metronidazole — single dose convenient for trichomoniasis
Metallic taste, disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol
Second/third trimester safe for treatment
drugs.source: WHO STI Guidelines 2022

localNames.my: မီဒ်ရောက်စီပ်ရိုဂျက်စတရယ် အာစီတိတ်
localNames.km: មេដ្រូក់ស៊ីប្រូហ្សេស្ត្រូន អាសេតាត

drugs.indication: Highly effective reversible contraception (injectable); prevents pregnancy for 12 weeks
drugs.dose: 150 mg IM q12 weeks; or 104 mg SC q13 weeks (sayana press)
drugs.timing: Every 12 weeks for IM; every 13 weeks for SC
contraindications: Current or history of breast cancer (relative)
warnings: Delays return to fertility (avg 9 months after last injection)
Weight gain and menstrual irregularities common — counsel on expectations
Safe in breastfeeding (no impact on milk production)
drugs.source: WHO MEC 5th ed. 2015; UNFPA Contraceptive Methods Guidance

localNames.my: အီသီနေးလ် အက်စတ្រាដিអოល + လီভိုနော်ဂျက်စတရယ်
localNames.km: ឌ្រា estradiol + levonorgestrel

drugs.indication: Oral contraception (combined); regular use prevents pregnancy; also used for menstrual regulation
drugs.dose: 0.03–0.035 mg ethinyl estradiol + 0.15 mg levonorgestrel daily
drugs.timing: One pill daily at same time; 7-day hormone-free interval for withdrawal bleed
warnings: WHO MEC Category 4: migraine with aura (stroke risk)
WHO MEC Category 2–3: smoking ≥15/day age ≥35, age ≥40
NO protection against STIs — counsel on condom use
drugs.source: WHO MEC 5th ed. 2015; UNFPA Contraceptive Methods Guidance

localNames.km: ឧបករណ៍ក្នុងប្រាក់ស្វាយ

drugs.indication: Long-acting reversible contraception (LARC); highly effective, minimal contraindications; also emergency contraception if inserted within 5 days
drugs.dose: Single insertion; effective 10+ years
drugs.timing: Can insert at any time in menstrual cycle if pregnancy ruled out; optimal timing 10–14 days after menses
warnings: Minimal medical contraindications — safe for most women
Ectopic pregnancy risk NOT increased
Can be inserted immediately post-abortion or postpartum (>4 weeks)
drugs.source: WHO MEC 5th ed. 2015; UNFPA Contraceptive Methods Guidance

localNames.my: လီဘိုနော်ဂျက်စတရယ်-မုံးတည့်
localNames.km: ឧបករណ៍ក្នុងប្រាក់ស្វាយដែលលេចចេញ levonorgestrel

drugs.indication: Long-acting reversible contraception (LARC); also used for heavy menstrual bleeding treatment; effective 3–7 years
drugs.dose: Single insertion (52 mg levonorgestrel)
drugs.timing: Can insert at any time if pregnancy ruled out; optimal 10–14 days after menses
warnings: Also reduces heavy menstrual bleeding by 50–90% — additional benefit
Ectopic pregnancy risk NOT increased
Can improve dysmenorrhea and reduce blood loss
drugs.source: WHO MEC 5th ed. 2015; UNFPA Contraceptive Methods Guidance

localNames.my: အယ်လ ကွန်ဒုံး
localNames.km: កុលសម្បទានបុរស

drugs.indication: Contraception AND STI prevention (only method with dual protection); barrier method
drugs.dose: One per act of intercourse
drugs.timing: Apply before intercourse; remove after ejaculation
drugs.alternativeDose: Female condom as alternative if male refuses
warnings: 98% effective with perfect use; 85% with typical use
ONLY method preventing both pregnancy AND STIs
User-dependent effectiveness — counsel on proper use and storage
drugs.source: WHO MEC 5th ed. 2015; UNFPA Contraceptive Methods Guidance

localNames.my: ယူလီပ်ရစ်စတယ် အာစီတိတ်
localNames.km: អូលីប្រីសថាល អាសេតាត

drugs.indication: Emergency contraception (more effective than levonorgestrel); effective up to 120 hours after intercourse
drugs.dose: 30 mg orally single dose
drugs.timing: Within 120 hours (5 days) of unprotected intercourse; more effective if given early
contraindications: Current or history of breast cancer (relative)
warnings: More effective than levonorgestrel (95% vs 60–90%) especially 72–120 hours
Requires prescription in some countries; over-the-counter in others
NOT a regular contraceptive — counsel on ongoing family planning
drugs.source: WHO MEC 5th ed. 2015; UNFPA Emergency Contraception Guidance

localNames.my: အင်္ဂါ အက်ငြီ
localNames.km: ឆ្នូលលោះអាស៊ីដ

drugs.indication: Heartburn/reflux in pregnancy; gastric upset
drugs.dose: 1–2 tablets q2–6h as needed (max 12 tablets/day)
drugs.timing: As needed
warnings: Safe in pregnancy; very minimal systemic absorption
May interfere with iron absorption — space dose 2 hours apart from iron
Counsel on avoiding other medications at same time
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: အိုင်ဗူပ်ရုဖင်
localNames.km: អាយបូប្រូហ្វេន

drugs.indication: Pain relief; fever reduction; dysmenorrhea (AVOID in pregnancy/postpartum)
drugs.dose: 200–400 mg q4–6h (max 1200 mg/day or 1800 mg/day with medical supervision)
drugs.timing: Every 4–6 hours as needed
contraindications: POSTPARTUM (risk of reduced placental blood flow)
warnings: AVOID in pregnancy — use paracetamol instead
Third trimester: increased risk of premature ductus arteriosus closure
Breastfeeding: minimal excretion — generally considered safe
drugs.source: WHO EML 23rd edition 2023; WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: အက်စပ်ရင်း
localNames.km: អាស្ពីរីន

drugs.indication: Low-dose for preeclampsia prevention (150–300 mg daily); pain relief (high-dose, NOT in pregnancy)
drugs.dose: 75–100 mg daily (preeclampsia prevention); 300–500 mg q4–6h (pain relief, non-pregnant)
drugs.timing: Once daily for preeclampsia prevention; every 4–6 hours for acute pain
warnings: Low-dose aspirin (75–100 mg) for preeclampsia prevention in high-risk women — safe
High-dose NSAID use in pregnancy: avoid especially third trimester
Combined with antihypertensives for preeclampsia management
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016; WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed.

localNames.my: ဗီတာမင် အေ
localNames.km: វីតាមីនក

drugs.indication: Supplementation in pregnancy (reduce maternal mortality in vitamin A-deficient regions); neonatal ophthalmia prophylaxis
drugs.dose: 10,000 IU daily or 25,000 IU weekly (pregnancy); 50,000 IU at delivery (single dose, endemic areas)
drugs.timing: Daily or weekly throughout pregnancy; single dose at delivery
warnings: Supplementation beneficial in vitamin A-deficient populations (South Asia, sub-Saharan Africa)
Reduces maternal and perinatal mortality in deficient populations by ~10–20%
NOT indicated in vitamin A-replete populations (Western countries, adequate diet)
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016

localNames.my: ကယ်လ်စီယမ် ကာබونိတ်
localNames.km: ស័ង្គម កាបូណាត

drugs.indication: Calcium supplementation in pregnancy (reduce hypertension and preeclampsia); prevention of osteoporosis
drugs.dose: 1000–1200 mg daily (elemental calcium) in divided doses
drugs.timing: Once or twice daily with food
warnings: Daily calcium supplementation reduces severe preeclampsia and maternal mortality by ~10–20% in low-calcium populations
Take with food for better absorption; separate from iron supplements (2 hours)
Safe in pregnancy and breastfeeding
drugs.source: WHO ANC Recommendations 2016; Cochrane review

localNames.my: ဆာဖာဒီယားဆင်း
localNames.km: ស៊ុលហ្វាឌីយ៉ាហ្សីន

drugs.indication: Congenital syphilis prevention/treatment (neonatal); alternative to penicillin in severe allergy
drugs.dose: 50 mg/kg q6h × 7–10 days (neonatal); 500 mg–1 g q6h (maternal penicillin allergy)
drugs.timing: Every 6 hours × 7–10 days
warnings: Avoid in newborns <2 months (sulfonamide kernicterus risk)
Maternal use in pregnancy: sulfonamide allergy assessment needed
Monitor for crystalluria — maintain adequate hydration
drugs.source: WHO STI Guidelines 2022

localNames.km: ត្រីមេតូផ្រីម-ស៊ុលហ្វាមេតូកស៊ាហ្សូល

drugs.indication: Urinary tract infection (uncomplicated); Pneumocystis prophylaxis (HIV); alternative for gonococcal resistance
drugs.dose: 160 mg/800 mg (1 DS tablet) q12h × 3–7 days (UTI); daily for prophylaxis
drugs.timing: Every 12 hours × 3–7 days
warnings: AVOID in first trimester (risk of neural tube defects)
AVOID late pregnancy (sulfonamide kernicterus risk in newborn)
Photosensitivity — counsel on sun protection
drugs.source: WHO STI Guidelines 2022; WHO EML 23rd edition

localNames.my: နီအို-ပလီမစ်ဆင်း-ဘယ်-ဗက်စီထရေးစင်း
localNames.km: ឆ្អូ minyak ឧបករណ៍សម្លាប់ក្រុមប៊ី

drugs.indication: Neonatal skin infection prophylaxis; minor cuts/abrasions; alternative to silver nitrate for ophthalmia prophylaxis
drugs.dose: Small amount applied to affected area; 1 drop in each eye for ophthalmia prophylaxis
drugs.timing: Once or twice daily for skin; once at birth for eyes
warnings: Topical route — minimal systemic absorption
Alternative to silver nitrate (which causes chemical conjunctivitis)
Not a substitute for maternal STI screening and treatment
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023; WHO EML 23rd edition

localNames.my: ကဖိန်း
localNames.km: កាហ្វេអ៊ីន

drugs.indication: Respiratory stimulant for apnea of prematurity; improves neonatal outcomes
drugs.dose: Loading: 20 mg/kg IV over 10–30 min; Maintenance: 5–10 mg/kg daily (q24h)
drugs.timing: Load once; then daily maintenance
warnings: Reduces apnea episodes by 80%+ in preterm infants
Improves neonatal outcomes (reduced BPD, death, neurodevelopmental impairment)
Half-life 4–6 days in neonates; monitor for toxicity (tachycardia, jitteriness)
drugs.source: WHO PCPNC 3rd ed. 2023; Cochrane review

localNames.my: ဒိုပါမင်း
localNames.km: ដូផាមីន

drugs.indication: Shock management (cardiogenic, hypovolemic after fluid resuscitation); low blood pressure support
drugs.dose: 2–5 mcg/kg/min (starting); titrate up to 5–10 mcg/kg/min (max 20 mcg/kg/min)
drugs.timing: Continuous infusion; titrate by effect
warnings: Requires continuous BP and HR monitoring
Central line recommended for peripheral administration >5 min
Risk of tachycardia and arrhythmias at higher doses
drugs.source: WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed. 2017

localNames.my: အီပီနဖရစ်း
localNames.km: អេពីណេហ្វ្រីន

drugs.indication: Anaphylaxis; severe allergy; cardiac arrest; severe asthma exacerbation
drugs.dose: 0.3–0.5 mg IM (1:1000 solution) for anaphylaxis; 1 mg IV q3–5 min for cardiac arrest
drugs.timing: Stat for anaphylaxis; every 3–5 min for cardiac arrest
contraindications: Coronary artery disease (relative; benefits may outweigh in emergency)
warnings: First-line for anaphylaxis — give IM to thigh for rapid absorption
IV route only in cardiac arrest or when IV already established
Risk of hypertension, tachycardia, myocardial ischemia
drugs.source: WHO EML 23rd edition 2023; WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed.

localNames.my: ထရန်း-အက်စမ်မစ်အက်ဆစ်
localNames.km: អាស៊ីដ tranexamic

drugs.indication: Severe postpartum hemorrhage (reduce mortality by ~30%); massive transfusion protocols
drugs.dose: 1 g IV loading dose, then 1 g IV q8h × 2 doses (within 3 hours of PPH onset)
drugs.timing: STAT on diagnosis of severe PPH (within 3 hours for efficacy)
warnings: WOMAN study showed 30% reduction in mortality if given <3 hours of PPH onset
Part of massive transfusion protocol alongside oxytocin, fluids, blood
Administer in hospital with blood bank capability
drugs.source: WOMAN Trial 2017; WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed.

localNames.my: ကာစ်ဘိုပ်ရစ်
localNames.km: ការប៉ូផ្រូស្ត

drugs.indication: Refractory PPH (when oxytocin and ergot fail); uterine atony
drugs.dose: 250 mcg IM q15 min (max 8 doses = 2 mg total)
drugs.timing: Every 15 minutes if needed
warnings: Use only after oxytocin and ergot failure — not first-line
More side effects (diarrhea, fever) than oxytocin
Effective for uterine atony when other agents fail
drugs.source: WHO IMPAC/MCPC 2nd ed. 2017
